data.content:
### Configure Claude Hooks and Tool Permissions Source: https://context7.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/llms.txt Centralized configuration file for managing Claude's hooks, permissions, and status line settings. It defines allowed/denied tools and configures various hook lifecycle events. ```json { "permissions": { "allow": [ "Bash(mkdir:*)", "Bash(uv:*)", "Write", "Edit", "Read" ], "deny": [] }, "statusLine": { "type": "command", "command": "uv run .claude/status_lines/status_line_v3.py", "padding": 0 }, "hooks": { "UserPromptSubmit": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "uv run .claude/hooks/user_prompt_submit.py --log-only --store-last-prompt --name-agent" }] }], "PreToolUse": [{ "matcher": "", "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "uv run .claude/hooks/pre_tool_use.py" }] }], "Stop": [{ "matcher": "", "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "uv run .claude/hooks/stop.py --chat" }] }] } } ``` -------------------------------- ### UserPromptSubmit Hook Configuration Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/README.md JSON configuration for the UserPromptSubmit hook in .claude/settings.json. It specifies the command to run the hook script, with options for logging, validation, and context injection. ```json "UserPromptSubmit": [ { "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "uv run .claude/hooks/user_prompt_submit.py --log-only" } ] } ] ``` -------------------------------- ### Example JSON Configuration for UserPromptSubmit Hook Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/user_prompt_submit_hook.md This JSON snippet shows an example configuration for the 'UserPromptSubmit' hook in a '.claude/settings.json' file. It defines two commands to be executed: one for logging prompts only and another for sending events with summarization. ```json { "hooks": { "UserPromptSubmit": [ { "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "uv run .claude/hooks/user_prompt_submit.py --log-only" }, { "type": "command", "command": "uv run .claude/hooks/send_event.py --source-app my-app --event-type UserPromptSubmit --summarize" } ] } ] } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure File Protection Hook Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/cc_hooks_docs.md Sets up a pre-tool-use hook to prevent edits on sensitive files. It uses a Python one-liner to check if the target file path contains sensitive patterns like '.env', 'package-lock.json', or '.git/'. ```json { "hooks": { "PreToolUse": [ { "matcher": "Edit|MultiEdit|Write", "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "python3 -c \"import json, sys; data=json.load(sys.stdin); path=data.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''); sys.exit(2 if any(p in path for p in ['.env', 'package-lock.json', '.git/']) else 0)\" } ] } ] } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Custom Notification Hook Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/cc_hooks_docs.md Configures a notification hook that triggers a desktop notification when Claude requires user input. This helps in timely intervention for interactive tasks. ```json { "hooks": { "Notification": [ { "matcher": "", "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "notify-send 'Claude Code' 'Awaiting your input'" } ] } ] } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Status Line Configuration JSON Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/README.md This JSON configuration file specifies the command to execute for the status line. It allows users to set their preferred status line script, such as 'status_line_v3.py', for their Claude Code environment. ```json { "StatusLine": { "command": "uv run .claude/status_lines/status_line_v3.py" } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Make Markdown Formatter Executable Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/cc_hooks_docs.md Command to grant execute permissions to the markdown_formatter.py script, allowing it to be run as a command. ```bash chmod +x .claude/hooks/markdown_formatter.py ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Markdown Formatting Hook Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/cc_hooks_docs.md Sets up a post-tool-use hook to automatically format markdown files. It triggers a Python script to fix language tags and spacing issues in markdown and MDX files. ```json { "hooks": { "PostToolUse": [ { "matcher": "Edit|MultiEdit|Write", "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/markdown_formatter.py" } ] } ] } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Claude Code Agent File Structure (YAML & Markdown) Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/README.md Defines the structure for Claude Code agent configuration files. It includes YAML frontmatter for metadata like name, description, tools, color, and model, followed by Markdown content for the agent's purpose, instructions, and response format. This structure is crucial for defining sub-agent behavior and enabling automatic delegation. ```yaml --- name: agent-name description: When to use this agent (critical for automatic delegation) tools: Tool1, Tool2, Tool3 # Optional - inherits all tools if omitted color: Cyan # Visual identifier in terminal model: opus # Optional - haiku | sonnet | opus - defaults to sonnet --- # Purpose You are a [role definition]. ## Instructions 1. Step-by-step instructions 2. What the agent should do 3. How to report results ## Report/Response Format Specify how the agent should communicate results back to the primary agent. ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Command Frontmatter Metadata Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/anthropic_custom_slash_commands.md This example shows how to use frontmatter in command files to define metadata. It includes `allowed-tools`, `argument-hint`, `description`, and `model`, providing essential configuration for the command's behavior and user experience. ```markdown --- allowed-tools: Bash(git add:*), Bash(git status:*), Bash(git commit:*) argument-hint: [message] description: Create a git commit model: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022 --- An example command ``` -------------------------------- ### Structured JSON Output for Hook Control (JSON) Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/user_prompt_submit_hook.md This JSON structure defines advanced output control for the UserPromptSubmit hook. It allows specifying a 'decision' (block/approve), a 'reason', additional 'context' to prepend, and whether to 'suppressOutput'. This provides fine-grained control over prompt processing. ```json { "decision": "block" | "approve" | undefined, "reason": "Explanation for the decision", "context": "Additional context to prepend to prompt", "suppressOutput": true | false } ``` -------------------------------- ### UserPromptSubmit Hook: Validate and Log User Prompts (Python) Source: https://context7.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/llms.txt Intercepts user prompts before Claude processes them for validation, security filtering, context injection, and audit logging. This hook can block prompts entirely by exiting with code 2. It logs prompt details to a file. ```python #!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --script # /// script # requires-python = ">=3.11" # dependencies = ["python-dotenv"] # /// import json import sys from pathlib import Path from datetime import datetime # Read JSON input from stdin input_data = json.load(sys.stdin) session_id = input_data.get('session', {}).get('id', 'unknown') prompt = input_data.get('prompt', '') # Log to file log_dir = Path("logs") log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) log_file = log_dir / 'user_prompt_submit.json' log_entry = { "timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z', "session_id": session_id, "prompt": prompt } existing_logs = [] if log_file.exists(): with open(log_file, 'r') as f: existing_logs = json.load(f) existing_logs.append(log_entry) with open(log_file, 'w') as f: json.dump(existing_logs, f, indent=2) # Block dangerous prompts (optional) if "rm -rf /" in prompt.lower(): print("BLOCKED: Dangerous system deletion command detected", file=sys.stderr) sys.exit(2) # Exit code 2 blocks the prompt sys.exit(0) # Success ``` -------------------------------- ### Subagent Configuration File Structure Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/anthropic_docs_subagents.md Defines the structure for subagent configuration files using Markdown with YAML frontmatter. It specifies required fields like 'name' and 'description', and optional fields like 'tools', along with their purpose and constraints. ```markdown --- name: your-sub-agent-name description: Description of when this subagent should be invoked tools: tool1, tool2, tool3 # Optional - inherits all tools if omitted --- Your subagent's system prompt goes here. This can be multiple paragraphs and should clearly define the subagent's role, capabilities, and approach to solving problems. Include specific instructions, best practices, and any constraints the subagent should follow. ``` -------------------------------- ### Log Bash Commands with jq Hook Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/cc_hooks_docs.md This hook intercepts 'PreToolUse' events for Bash commands. It uses `jq` to extract the command and its description, logging them to a file. This allows for tracking executed commands. ```json { "hooks": { "PreToolUse": [ { "matcher": "Bash", "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "jq -r '\"\(.tool_input.command) - \(.tool_input.description // \"No description\")\"' >> ~/.claude/bash-command-log.txt" } ] } ] } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Set Custom Output Style to GenUI for HTML Formatting Source: https://context7.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/llms.txt Configures Claude to use the 'genui' output style, which generates HTML responses with embedded styling and JavaScript. This is ideal for creating interactive visualizations and rich presentations. ```yaml --- name: genui description: Generates beautiful HTML with embedded styling --- # GenUI Output Style Generate interactive HTML outputs with embedded CSS and JavaScript. Perfect for data visualizations, dashboards, and rich presentations. ## Usage ```bash /output-style genui # Now ask: "Create a dashboard showing project statistics" # Claude generates self-contained HTML file with: # - Embedded CSS styling # - Interactive JavaScript # - Responsive design # - Ready to open in browser ``` ## Example Output ```html

Project Statistics

156 Files
12,453 Lines
``` ``` -------------------------------- ### PreToolUse Hook: Block Dangerous Commands (Python) Source: https://context7.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/llms.txt Executes before any tool call to block dangerous operations. It validates commands, specifically targeting variations of `rm -rf` and preventing access to `.env` files (except `.env.sample`) to enhance security. ```python #!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --script # /// script # requires-python = ">=3.8" # /// import json import sys import re def is_dangerous_rm_command(command): """Detect dangerous rm -rf variations.""" normalized = ' '.join(command.lower().split()) patterns = [ r'\brm\s+.*-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f', # rm -rf, rm -fr r'\brm\s+.*-[a-z]*f[a-z]*r', # rm -fr variations r'\brm\s+--recursive\s+--force', ] for pattern in patterns: if re.search(pattern, normalized): return True return False # Read JSON input input_data = json.load(sys.stdin) tool_name = input_data.get('tool_name', '') tool_input = input_data.get('tool_input', {}) # Block dangerous rm commands if tool_name == 'Bash': command = tool_input.get('command', '') if is_dangerous_rm_command(command): print("BLOCKED: Dangerous rm command detected", file=sys.stderr) print(f"Command: {command}", file=sys.stderr) sys.exit(2) # Exit code 2 blocks tool execution # Block .env file access (except .env.sample) if tool_name in ['Read', 'Edit', 'Write']: file_path = tool_input.get('file_path', '') if '.env' in file_path and not file_path.endswith('.env.sample'): print("BLOCKED: Access to .env files prohibited", file=sys.stderr) sys.exit(2) sys.exit(0) ``` -------------------------------- ### Cryptocurrency Analyzer Agent Configuration Source: https://context7.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/llms.txt Configuration for a specialized agent designed to analyze cryptocurrency market data, news sentiment, and technical indicators. It utilizes WebSearch, Bash, and Write tools for data gathering and reporting. ```yaml --- name: crypto-coin-analyzer-sonnet description: Cryptocurrency analysis specialist for real-time market insights. Use proactively when given one specific crypto ticker symbol to analyze current price, news, sentiment, and technical indicators. tools: WebSearch, Bash, Write color: blue model: sonnet --- # Purpose You are a cryptocurrency analysis specialist focused on providing comprehensive, real-time insights for a single cryptocurrency. ## Instructions 1. **Validate Input**: Confirm you received a specific crypto ticker (BTC, ETH, etc.) 2. **Market Data Analysis** (parallel execution): - Current price and 24h change - Market cap and trading volume - Technical indicators (RSI, moving averages) 3. **News & Sentiment**: - Recent news articles (last 24-48 hours) - Social media sentiment analysis - Major developments or announcements 4. **Risk Assessment**: - Volatility metrics - Support/resistance levels - Market correlation factors 5. **Generate Report**: Write comprehensive analysis to `analysis_{TICKER}_{TIMESTAMP}.md` ## Report Format ```markdown # {COIN_NAME} ({TICKER}) Analysis Date: {TIMESTAMP} ## Market Overview - Price: $X.XX (±Y%) - Market Cap: $XXB - 24h Volume: $XXM ## Technical Analysis - RSI: XX (Overbought/Neutral/Oversold) - Key Levels: Support $XX / Resistance $XX ## News & Sentiment - [Headlines and sentiment summary] ## Risk Assessment - Volatility: High/Medium/Low - Recommendation: Buy/Hold/Sell (with rationale) ``` ## Usage Example User: "Analyze BTC" Primary Agent → Delegates to crypto-coin-analyzer-sonnet Sub-agent → Executes analysis → Reports back Primary Agent → Presents results to user ``` -------------------------------- ### Basic Logging Implementation (Python) Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/user_prompt_submit_hook.md This Python script implements the UserPromptSubmit hook for basic logging. It reads prompt data from stdin, saves it to a session-specific JSON log file, and exits successfully. It handles directory creation and file appending logic. ```python #!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --script # /// script # requires-python = ">=3.11" # /// import json import sys from pathlib import Path # Read input input_data = json.loads(sys.stdin.read()) session_id = input_data.get('session_id', 'unknown') prompt = input_data.get('prompt', '') # Log to file log_dir = Path(f"logs/{session_id}") log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) log_file = log_dir / 'user_prompts.json' # Append prompt if log_file.exists(): with open(log_file, 'r') as f: prompts = json.load(f) else: prompts = [] prompts.append({ 'timestamp': input_data.get('timestamp'), 'prompt': prompt }) with open(log_file, 'w') as f: json.dump(prompts, f, indent=2) sys.exit(0) ``` -------------------------------- ### Ollama Setup for Local LLM Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/README.md Instructions for setting up Ollama to use local LLM models for Claude Code. This includes downloading Ollama, pulling a specific model like 'gpt-oss:20b', and starting the Ollama server. ```bash # Install Ollama from https://ollama.com/download # Pull the GPT-OSS model (13GB) ollama pull gpt-oss:20b # Start Ollama server ollama serve ``` -------------------------------- ### Log User Prompt to Session Directory (Python) Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/ai_docs/user_prompt_submit_hook.md This Python snippet demonstrates how to log user prompts to a session-specific directory. It reads a JSON payload from stdin, extracts session ID and prompt details, and appends them to a JSON log file. Ensures the log directory exists and handles file creation/writing. ```python # Log user prompt to session directory log_dir = ensure_session_log_dir(session_id) log_file = log_dir / 'user_prompt_submit.json' # Append to existing log log_data.append(input_data) with open(log_file, 'w') as f: json.dump(log_data, f, indent=2) ``` -------------------------------- ### Comprehensive Crypto Research Orchestration Source: https://context7.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/llms.txt Configuration for a custom command '/crypto_research' that orchestrates multiple cryptocurrency agents in parallel to perform comprehensive market analysis, identify investment opportunities, and analyze macro correlations. It uses the 'Task' tool. ```yaml --- allowed-tools: Task description: Execute comprehensive cryptocurrency research using all crypto agents --- # Crypto Research Launch specialized crypto agents in parallel for complete market analysis. ``` -------------------------------- ### JSON: Common Hook Control Fields Source: https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery/blob/main/README.md This JSON structure outlines common fields that can be returned by various Claude Code hooks to control execution flow and output. Fields include 'continue' to determine if Claude should proceed, 'stopReason' for messages when stopping, and 'suppressOutput' to hide stdout. ```json { "continue": true, // Whether Claude should continue (default: true) "stopReason": "string", // Message when continue=false (shown to user) "suppressOutput": true // Hide stdout from transcript (default: false) } ```
